heading: Black Monday 1987
info: A 22.6% drop in a single trading session.
text: Weeks before Black Monday in 1987, the market's internal mathematics changed. It began to 'remember' its recent state more than usual.

heading: Statistical Flickers
info: Rising skewness detected in 2000 and 2008 crises.
text: Using cumulative returns, researchers found rising variance and skewness preceded both the 2000 Dot-com crash and the 2008 Lehman Brothers bankruptcy.

heading: CSD in Markets
info: Market fluctuations grow larger near bifurcation points.
text: Critical Slowing Down in markets is a loss of resilience. The market's fluctuations grow larger as it approaches a bifurcation point.

heading: The Feedback Paradox
info: Detection can trigger the very event it warns of.
text: But social systems are harder than nature: if everyone sees the warning signal, their fear may trigger the very crash they were trying to avoid.
